The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: ELEN MEDER VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA - HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 8, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER ELEN MEDER Notice Of Hearing "Writ". Plaintiff Ellen Meder's petition for writ of mandate is granted in part. This case is remanded to the California Department of Social Services with directions to give Ms. Meder the opportunity to provide evidence showing that she is entitled to a larger amount of money per the Cash Assistance Program for Immigrants for the period October 2017 through July 2018 than previously determined by the Department. Using the independent judgment standard of review, Ms. Meder has not shown that the prior determination of the Department was erroneous. However, it appears that Ms. Meder may be able to provide evidence that she is entitled to additional monies and the Court and the Department believe that she should be given an opportunity to provide such evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ROSA RODRIGUEZ VS. GEAR UP COMMUNICATIONS INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Alternatively To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery And Production Of Documents Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT GEAR UP COMMUNICATIONS INC.'s Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Alternatively To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery And Production Of Documents Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steve Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions and alternatively to compel plaintiffs responses to written discovery and production of documents request for monetary sanctions is unopposed and granted to the extent plaintiff shall respond to Supplemental Interrogatories and Supplemental Request for Document within twenty days of service of this order with notice of entry. Monetary sanctions in the sum of $610.00 is awarded to defendant to be paid within the aforesaid twenty days. Noted is the action is set for a dismissal hearing in Department 610 at 10:30 am today. If the action is dismissed today this order is moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 8, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, LISABETH CASTRO-SMYTH, JULIE GOOT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues. Defendants San Francisco Unified School District, Valerie Hoshino, Lisabeth Castro-Smyth, and Julie Goot's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. As to the breach of mandatory duty claim, the court determines that CANRA required that a report be made on behalf of plaintiff John Doe as a victim and there is a triable dispute whether the abuse report was made on John Doe's behalf. (Goot Dec., 4; Castro-Smyth Dec., 5; Hoshino Depo., 91:11-18; Goot Depo., 98:9-13.) The report lists another student-not plaintiff-as the victim. (Walker Dec., Ex. 6.) Defendants fail to satisfy their summary judgment burden on the causation/damages issue. Defendants do not present evidence establishing that their failure to file a required report did not cause harm to John Doe. Defendants solely rely on the deposition testimony of John Doe's mother that she does not know how a report to CPS would have improved the situation. (Jane Depo., 277:2-4.) That testimony fails to show that damages "cannot be established." (CCP 437c(p)(2).) Defendants owed a duty to reasonably monitor the children. (J.H. v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 123, 142 [ "[a] special relationship is formed between a school district and its students resulting in the imposition of an affirmative duty on the school district to take all reasonable steps to protect its students [and t]his affirmative duty arises, in part, based on the compulsory nature of education."] (internal citations omitted; emphasis in original); Dailey v. Los Angeles Unified Sch. Dist. (1970) 2 Cal.3d 741, 747 ["California law has long imposed on school authorities a duty to 'supervise at all times the conduct of the children on the school grounds and to enforce those rules and regulations necessary to their protection.'"].) There are triable issues of fact whether defendants breached that duty and whether the breach caused harm to John Doe. John Doe testified that the schoolyard monitors were on their phones at the time of the incident. (John Doe Depo., 56:12-57:1; 57:23-58:21.) There is sufficient evidence on this motion to create a triable issue that, had the monitors been attentive, the harm to John Doe would not have occurred or would have been reduced. Defendants' discretionary immunity argument does not completely dispose of an entire cause of action and thus is improper on this motion. (CCP 437c(f)(1).) The individual defendants are being sued for their own derelictions-not the conduct of others-and therefore Gov't Code § 820.8 does not bar the claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS P. GALICIA VS. WESTFIELD GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses And Document Production Of Emporium Mall, Llc.. To Dtpd#1 And Request For Sanctions...
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF LUIS GALICIA's Motion To Compel Further Responses And Document Production Of Emporium Mall, LLC. To Demand for Production (Set No. One) And Request of Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steve Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel production of the incident report is denied without prejudice. The written agreement between security contractor and Professional Security Consultants ("PSC") and Emporium Mall provides the "Incident Report shall be completed and such incident reports shall remain the property of the manager". Plaintiff's argument an independently retained security contractor's report to its principal cannot per se be a protected privileged communication is overruled. Defendant Emporium Mall's has sustained its burden of establishing the incident report is subject to the attorney-client privilege. With PSC presently a party to the action discovery may be undertaken to obtain facts to support the allegation the dominant purpose of the report was not litigation. Both parties' request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GENOVEVA BAUTISTA VS. JAMIE WESTON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 8, 2019, Line 8, PLAINTIFF GENOVEVA BAUTISTA, MARIA BAUTISTA DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Plaintiffs Genoveva and Mary Bautista's demurrer to Defendants Jamie Weston and Eric Gigliotti's answer is overruled in its entirety. The primary function of the answer is to give the other party notice so that it may prepare its case. (Harris v. City of Santa Monica (2013) 56 Cal.4th 203, 240.) A purported defect in a pleading that otherwise gives notice to the other side cannot be said to affect substantial rights or cause prejudice. (Id.) If, after a reasonable opportunity for discovery and factual investigation, Plaintiffs contend that Defendants lack factual support for some or all of the affirmative defenses, Plaintiffs should request that Defendants withdraw those affirmative defenses. If Plaintiffs are dissatisfied by the response, then they may bring a summary adjudication motion seeking to eliminate the affirmative defenses lacking factual support. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: HERCULES MOSS VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tranfer Venue From The County Of San Francisco Superior Court To Sacramento County Superior Court; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Chase R. Morello
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 8, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT JULIE MESMAN Motion To Tranfer Venue From The County Of San Francisco Superior Court To Sacramento County Superior Court. Defendant Julie Anne Mesman's motion to change venue is denied. Defendant's motion to change venue under Code Civ. Proc. § 397(a)'s mandatory grounds is denied. On a motion to change venue, the burden is on the moving party to show (1) that venue is proper in a different county than the one in which the lawsuit was filed and (2) that venue is improper in the current county under any applicable theory. (La Mirada Community Hospital v. Superior Court (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 39, 42.) Although Defendant has established that Sacramento County would be a proper venue, it has failed to show that venue is improper in San Francisco County under any applicable theory. Under Code of Civ. Proc. § 395(a) an action for personal injury is proper "in either the county where the injury occurs or the injury causing death occurs or the county where the defendants, or some of them reside." Although Defendant Mesman does not reside in San Francisco County, Defendant Uber Technologies' principal place of business is. (Kang Decl. ISO Opp., Ex. C.) Thus, venue is proper in San Francisco. (Code Civ. Proc. § 395.5 ["A corporation may be sued in the county . . . the principal place of business of such corporation is situated"].) Defendant's motion to change venue under Code Civ. Proc. § 397(c)' discretionary grounds is denied. Under a § 397(c) motion, "only convenience to third-party disinterested witnesses" may be considered. (Corfee, 202 Cal.App.2d at 478.) The convenience of the parties may not be considered. Thus, defendant's primary reliance on the purported inconvenience of the San Francisco forum to itself is misplaced. Defendant does not sufficiently identify or establish inconvenience of any other witness. Such a showing must be done "through affidavits which contain something more than generalities and conclusions." (Corfee, 202 Cal.App.2d at 477.) "[T]he supporting declarations must show (1) the name of each witness, (2) the expected testimony of each witness, and (3) facts showing why the attendance of said witnesses at trial will be inconvenient at trial and why the ends of justice would be served by a transfer." (Stute v. Burinda (1981) 123 Cal.App.Supp.3d 11, 17.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
